title: Published package omits the harness entrypoint that consumers and tests expect
reasoning: The package manifest only publishes `bin/`, `dist/cli.mjs`, `dist/sdk.mjs`, and SDK type files. It does not export `./harness` and does not include `dist/harness.mjs` or `src/entrypoints/harness.ts` in the packed file list. That means a real npm consumer cannot import `@gitlawb/openclaude/harness`, even though the package-consumer test suite explicitly models and compiles that import path as intended behavior.
recommendation: Add a real `./harness` export in `package.json` and include the corresponding built artifact and type source in `files`, or remove the harness consumer test if the entrypoint is not meant to ship.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current package-export test only checks `./sdk` and `./dist/cli.mjs` on the real `package.json`. The harness test builds a synthetic package.json inside a temp directory, so it does not verify the actual published manifest or tarball contents.
suggestedRegressionTest: Assert the real `package.json` contains `exports['./harness']` and `files` includes `dist/harness.mjs` and `src/entrypoints/harness.ts`, then run the consumer compilation against `npm pack` output instead of a handcrafted temp package.
minimumFixScope: package.json and the package-consumer-types test if the harness entrypoint is intentionally part of the release surface.
